_README: Per-vehicle briefing overrides for generate_pdf_report.py (kept in the skill, not vehicles.json, for self-containment). ARTIC GVM TIERS — domain knowledge for the load points: an articulated HGV's gross vehicle mass falls into tiers, lightest to heaviest — (1) TRACTOR-ONLY / bobtail ~10 t = the lightest cluster, NOT a real operating mass -> EXCLUDE it (set 'unladen_band_t' lo above it); (2) TRACTOR + EMPTY TRAILER ~16-17 t = UNLADEN; (3) LOADED operations = higher, operation-dependent, up to the rated GVW (~42 t) = LADEN. Set the band by AI JUDGEMENT of the observed GVM histogram/KDE, NOT by the default KDE split (which merges the ~17 t unladen shoulder into the dominant laden peak and so mislabels a mostly-laden vehicle's unladen as ~19-20 t). For such artics set 'unladen_band_t': [lo, hi] (and optionally 'laden_min_t'): unladen = data points in [lo, hi) (tractor + empty trailer), laden = GVM >= laden_min (default hi), bobtail (< lo) excluded. Omit to fall back to KDE light/heavy clustering. 'unladen_mass_t' can pin the unladen marker mass explicitly. 'note' records the judgement + provenance.

EX74JXW.note: Scania 23P, type ARTIC, rated GVW 42 t (SRF weightClass=42, 518 kWh). Tractor solo (bobtail) ~12 t — the ~10-14 t data points — is NOT 'unladen'. UNLADEN = tractor + empty trailer ~16-17 t = the 14-20 t data points; LADEN = loaded operation, GVM >= 20 t (median ~24-25 t); full = 42 t rated GVW (observed GVM tops out ~36 t, so 42 t is a dashed projection). Bands set by AI judgement of the artic configuration, grounded in the observed GVM distribution.

CMZ6260.note: Volvo FH Electric, ARTIC, rated GVW ~42 t. AI judgement of the all-data GVM histogram (n=327, KDE peaks 10.5 / 19.6 / 23.8 t): three tiers — bobtail tractor-only ~10.5 t (the 10-12 t bin, 12 pts) EXCLUDED; UNLADEN tractor + empty trailer = the 14-18 t band (~27 pts, data median ~16.4 t) — marker PINNED to 17 t (the canonical tractor+trailer figure; data supports ~16-17 t); LADEN = loaded operations GVM >= 18 t (dominant runs 18-26 t, median ~22 t); full = 42 t rated GVW (observed tops ~26 t bar one 46 t outlier, so 42 t is a dashed projection). The DEFAULT KDE split mislabels this mostly-laden vehicle — it merges the ~17 t unladen shoulder into the dominant ~19.6 t laden peak, giving a wrong unladen ~19 t; the band corrects unladen to ~17 t.
